data.title: MarketAxess reports 44% higher ADV in May
data.description:
Electronic trading platform MarketAxess (NASDAQ:MKTX) announced on Thursday a trading average daily volume of $46.23B in May, up 44% from the prior-year period and down 20% month-over-month.

Credit ADV rose 22% on a yearly basis and fell 11% from the previous month to $16.35B. Rates ADV increased 59% year-over-year and declined 24% from a month ago to $29.88B.

Eurobonds ADV reached $2.9B on a 116% increase in block trading and a 500% increase in portfolio trading ADV.

data.title: Avricore appoints Rodger Seccombe as CEO
data.description:
• Avricore (AVCR:CA) Thursday named Rodger Seccombe as CEO of the company. 
  • Seccombe currently serves as Chief Technology Officer of Avricore and is the President and Co-Founder of its wholly owned subsidiary, HealthTab.
  • Hector Bremner has resigned from his position as CEO and director of the company.

data.title: Networking stocks mixed after Ciena's mixed Q1 results
data.description:
• Networking stocks were mixed in premarket trading on Thursday after Ciena (NYSE:CIEN) reported mixed first-quarter results compared to Wall Street's forecast.
  • Ciena shares fell 5.5% in premarket trading, as the Gary Smith-led company earned an adjusted $0.42 per share during the period, $0.10 worse than the consensus estimate. However, revenue for the period jumped 24.1% year-over-year to $1.13B, $40M above the consensus. Sales for the quarter were driven by two customers, which accounted for 23.9% of total revenue. 
  • Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) bucked the decline and rose 0.4% in premarket trading, while Cisco (NASDAQ:CSCO) fell 0.1%. Juniper Networks (NYSE:JNPR), which is still in the process of being acquired by HP Enterprise (HPE), was little changed.
  • Ciena is set to host a conference call at 8:30 a.m. EST to discuss the results.

data.title: Flow Beverage CFO steps down
data.description:
• Flow Beverage (TSX:FLOW:CA) (OTCQX:FLWBF) said that Trent MacDonald has stepped down from his position as CFO and executive VP of Operations.
  • The Board of Directors has initiated a process to select a new CFO and is in advanced discussions with a preferred candidate for this position.
  • In the interim, the company’s Director of Finance will perform similar functions to those of the CFO. 
  • Mr. MacDonald will assist with this transition before he moves.

data.title: European markets in green as investors await ECB interest rate decision
data.description:
Update: The revised post updates market movements as of 7.20am ET. 

London (UKX) +0.31% to 8,828, after trading almost flat for three days as mixed corporate news weigh investor sentiment.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.29% to 24,348, surpassing 24,300 to hit a new milestone and extend its winning streak to three consecutive days. The HCOB Germany Construction PMI slipped to 44.4 in May. Factory orders in Germany rose 0.6% M/M in April.

France (CAC:IND) +0.37% to 7,833, The HCOB Construction PMI in France fell to 43.1 in May.

The HCOB Eurozone Construction PMI fell to 45.6 in May.

The HCOB Italy Construction PMI edged up to 50.5 in May.

Finland’s trade deficit narrowed to €0.10B in April. Slovenia posted a trade deficit of €446.5M in April.

Sweden’s current account surplus widened sharply to SEK 119.3B in Q1.

The Swiss unemployment rate remained stable at a non-seasonally adjusted 2.8% in May.

Wholesale prices in Austria fell by 0.5% Y/Y in May.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) gained 0.36% to 553, as investors refrained from making big bets ahead of the European Central Bank’s monetary policy decision later in the day. European natural gas futures climbed above €36 per megawatt-hour as supply from Norway, the region’s top gas exporter, dropped due to planned maintenance at the Kollsnes processing plant.

Coming up in the session: ECB interest rate decision is lined up for the day, where it expected to cut interest rates by another 25 basis points at its June meeting as it seeks to support growth amid slowing inflation and ongoing global trade uncertainties.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down basis point to 4.35%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 3 basis points to 2.49%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 2 basis points to 4.58%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Dine Brands prices $600M senior notes financing
Metalpha partners with DogeOS to expand crypto innovation
data.description:
Metalpha Technology (NASDAQ:MATH) Thursday announced a partnership with DogeOS—the application layer founded by the MyDoge team—to accelerate Dogecoin's (DOGE) evolution.

This collaboration unites Metalpha's institutional support for proof-of-work (PoW) ecosystems with DogeOS's vision to transform DOGE into a versatile blockchain platform for gaming, DeFi, and AI.

Through this partnership, Metalpha will bring institutional liquidity that may enter productive use cases within the DogeOS ecosystem.

data.title: Neuberger Berman raises $4 billion for latest GP-led secondary fund
data.description:
Neuberger Berman Thursday announced the final close of NB Strategic Capital Fund II at just over $4 billion of total capital commitments, with additional capital committed by limited partners to co-invest alongside the Fund.

The amount surpassed the Fund's initial target of $2.5 billion and represents a four-fold increase from the predecessor fund, NB Strategic Capital Fund I, which held a final close in 2020 at just over $955 million.

The Fund's diverse investor base spans North America, Europe, Asia and Latin America, featuring corporate and public pension plans, endowments, foundations, insurance companies, family offices, and high net worth individuals.

data.title: 23andMe seeks second auction as ex-CEO outbids Regeneron
data.description:
Bankrupt DNA testing company 23andMe Holding (OTC:MEHCQ) is set to hold a second auction to sell its assets, including customers’ genetic data, after a group led by its former CEO Anne Wojcicki submitted a $305M bid, beating an initial $256M offer from Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN).

Following its Chapter 11 filing in a Missouri bankruptcy court in March, South San Francisco, California-based 23andMe (OTC:MEHCQ) picked Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN) as the successful bidder in the first auction held last month.

Wojcicki cited irregularities in the initial auction, arguing that it was unfairly closed to favor the Tarrytown, New York-based biotech.

However, a subsequent bid from TTAM Research Institute, a California-based nonprofit founded by Wojcicki, has prompted the company to seek permission from U.S. Bankruptcy Judge Brian Walsh in St. Louis, Missouri, to seek fresh offers from potential buyers.

According to Reuters, Regeneron’s (NASDAQ:REGN) attorney Emil Kleinhaus told the judge that his client wants to submit a fresh offer but requires a $10M breakup fee if MEHCQ eventually accepts TTAM Research’s bid.

At stake is data representing more than 15M consumer DNA profiles, collected from 23andMe's (OTC:MEHCQ) popular saliva-based at-home DNA testing kits. Any transaction will exclude 23andMe’s Lemonaid Health business, which the company intends to wind down.

data.title: Asana gets rating cut at HSBC after quarterly results
data.description:
HSBC downgraded Asana (NYSE:ASAN) on Wednesday to Reduce from Hold and cut the price target on the stock to $10 from $13.

Analysts led by Stephen Bersey said they have cut their fiscal 2026-29 revenue estimates for Asana by 2-5%. The analysts added that the company’s revenue growth decelerated to 8.6% year-over-year in the first quarter of fiscal 2026 (in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025 it was 10%), and fiscal year 2026 guidance suggests further deceleration to about 8% year-over-year (7.5%, excluding foreign exchange, or FX).

The analysts added that Asana's management comments during the call pointed to continued pressures in the core technology segment and weakness in enterprise and middle-market segments.

Dollar-based net revenue retention rate dipped to 95% in the first quarter of fiscal 2026 (in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025 it was 96%) and management expects further decline in the fiscal second quarter, the analysts added.

Bersey and his team said that the company reported good traction for its AI Studio offering, but reached an annual recurring revenue rate of $1M, which is too small to move the needle.

The analysts believe declining growth, pricing pressure, and sub-100 net revenue retention points to continued poor performance and indicates that the company’s total addressable market, or TAM, and customer Return on Invested Capital, or ROIC, may be more limited than management had previously expected. This is concerning given the relatively low revenue base, the analysts added.

Shares of Asana fell about 20% on Wednesday. The stock was largely flat premarket on Thursday.
